name: Chocolate Spiderweb Snaps
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Southern Living
Cream the shortening until light and fluffy using an electric mixer.
Gradually add sugar, beating at medium speed.
Add melted chocolate, eggs, corn syrup, water, and vanilla; mix well.
Combine flour, baking soda, and salt; stir into the creamed mixture until just blended.
Shape dough into two 12-inch rolls; wrap in wax paper. Chill several hours.
Unwrap rolls, and cut into 1/4-inch slices. Place on ungreased cookie sheets.
Bake at 350 degrees for 10 to 12 minutes. Cool on cookie sheets for 5 minutes. Transfer to wire racks to cool completely.
Spread frosting over cookies, leaving a 1/8-inch edge from the edges. Let stand until frosting sets.
For a spiderweb pattern, place chocolate morsels in a double boiler and melt until smooth. Let almost set but not firm.
Spoon melted chocolate into a decorating bag fitted with a metal tip No. 2.
For round pattern, pipe circles around the top of the cookie, spacing evenly.
Pull the point of a wooden pick from center to outer edge of each circle.
For linear pattern, pipe parallel lines about 1/4-inch apart across the top of the cookie. Pull the point of a wooden pick diagonally across the lines.
Let stand at room temperature until chocolate is firm.
Yield: 6 dozen
Frosting: Combine sugar and water to desired spreading consistency. Color with paste food coloring as desired. Yield: 1 3/4 cups.
NOTE: Dough may be frozen up to 3 months. Slice while frozen, and bake as directed.
